description: The provided image is an unreadable low-resolution placeholder displaying a 'Check for updates' icon, containing no verifiable scientific data, micrographs, spectra, or diffraction patterns related to the studied steel. Direct pixel-level observation of precipitate morphology, crystal structure, or elemental distribution is impossible. The accompanying text extraction summary indicates that the paper examines co-precipitation in Fe-28Mn-10Al-1C-3Cu austenitic low-density steel after solution treatment at 1000 °C for 1 h and aging at 550 °C for 3 h, referencing figures that should contain SAED patterns, TEM micrographs, EDS maps, and HRTEM images of Cu-rich phase and κ-carbide precipitates. Key text-reported findings include coherent precipitates with specific orientation relationships, lattice parameters (austenite a = 0.3689 nm, κ-carbide a = 0.3775 nm, Cu-rich phase a = 0.3652 nm), and precipitate dimensions (~10 nm irregular ovals and ~30 nm bars), yet none of these details can be visually confirmed from the current image. Consequently, the figure-level scientific conclusions regarding co-precipitation mechanisms remain dependent on caption and text statements rather than direct image evidence.
